response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. To get through a leap year, she needs to calculate how many days there are in a leap year and then determine how many ounces of shampoo she will need. A leap year has 366 days, so she will use shampoo 366 / 2 = 183 times. This means she needs 183 * (1/4) = 45.75 ounces of shampoo. Since she can't buy a fraction of a bottle, she will need to round up to the next whole number, which is 46 ounces. She buys shampoo in 14-ounce bottles, so she will need [46 / 14] = [3.2857] bottles. Since she can't buy a fraction of a bottle, she will need [4] bottles to get through the leap year.

Brittany will need [4] bottles of shampoo to get through a leap year. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so she will use [4 * 14] = [56] ounces of shampoo. This will cover her needs for 366 days, as she uses 1/4 ounce every other day. She will have [56 - 45.75] = [10.25] ounces of shampoo left over.
